description: Agent Bridge Protocol (ABP) + reusable connector/SDK. Protocol-first. See DESIGN.md and SPEC/abp-v1.md.
rules: One feature per session. Commit, update progress.txt, exit.
Never set status:passed without test output recorded in the session.
Never edit/remove existing feature entries; only append or flip status.
On a broken state, git reset to last green commit; do not patch on decay.
Reference implementation language: TypeScript (Node), schema validation via a JSON Schema validator.

features.title: ABP v1 normative spec
features.evidence: SPEC/abp-v1.md written; covers envelope, control+data planes, closed schemas, security model, conformance.
steps: Cover lifecycle, security invariants, errors, conformance, extensibility

features.title: Machine-readable closed schemas (envelope/event/action/error)
features.evidence: SPEC/schemas/{envelope,event,action,error}.json written; all parse as valid JSON (init.sh check). Superseded/relocated by F0.3 (layered: schemas/core/ + schemas/profiles/).
steps: Author JSON Schema 2020-12 for the security-critical closed types

features.title: Layered architecture revision (Core + World Profile) per design review
features.evidence: Adopted plan B (layering) after foundational review. SPEC/abp-v1.md rewritten: §0.3 Core/Profile split; §5 World Profiles (profile meta-schema, x-abp-trust trust annotation, open-object bounds, content-hash pinning no fetch-by-URL); resume event-seq cursor (§4.3.2); in-band roles_query/roles_list + claim-based bind authorization + host-pubkey pinning (§4.2); relative deadlines + idempotency (§4.3.3); message size/depth limits (§2); error codes profile_unsupported/profile_mismatch. Schemas relocated to schemas/core/{envelope,event,action,error,profile}.json + schemas/profiles/social/1.json. init.sh recurses. Verification: all 6 schemas parse (init.sh); social profile passes structural check against core/profile.json meta-schema; untrusted + client_authored annotations present (python structural check). External refs §0.2/§6/§9 preserved.
steps: Rewrite SPEC/abp-v1.md: layered arch, profiles, trust annotation, resume cursor, role discovery+claim, relative deadlines, idempotency, size/depth bounds, host pinning.
Relocate schemas to core/ + profiles/social/1.json; add core/profile.json meta-schema; add x-abp-trust + context bounds.
init.sh recurse; verify parse + meta-structural check.

features.title: Schema validator + remaining control-plane schemas + conformance tests
features.evidence: Authored all 10 remaining control-plane schemas in SPEC/schemas/core/ (hello, hello_ack [inlined profile + hash], roles_query, roles_list, pair_challenge/request/result, resume [last_event_seq], ping [shared with pong], bye). Built packages/validator (TypeScript, Node 24 type-stripping, ajv 2020-12): CoreValidator validates the closed envelope, dispatches payload by type, enforces §2 size(65536B)/depth(16) limits; data-plane validates Core event/action envelope only (profile composition deferred to F1.2); x-abp-trust registered as a no-op annotation keyword. Conformance suite: 25/25 pass (node --test) + tsc typecheck clean. Verified via ./init.sh: 16 schemas parse, typecheck clean, 25 pass / 0 fail. Covered: valid messages pass; unknown type/kind-field rejected; additionalProperties:false enforced; data-plane+resume require session; event requires seq; wrong abp version rejected; oversize/non-JSON/over-depth/non-object rejected. Fixed envelope.json comment->$comment and added resume to session-required set.
steps: Author remaining control-plane schemas (hello, hello_ack, roles_query, roles_list, pair_*, resume, ping, bye) per §4 field tables (hello_ack carries the inlined profile + hash; resume carries last_event_seq).
Validator lib that checks envelope + payload by type/kind (Core layer only here; profile composition is F1.2).
Conformance tests: unknown type/kind/fields rejected; valid messages pass; data-plane requires session; size/depth limits (§2) enforced.

features.title: World Profile loader + pinning + composed data-plane validation
features.evidence: packages/validator/src/profile.ts: ProfileLoader validates a profile doc against core/profile.json meta-schema and compiles every events[kind].data / actions[kind].data; canonicalize()+profileHash() give a stable sha256-base64url content hash (§5.5); pin() verifies recomputed hash == advertised (else profile_mismatch), checks document self-identifies as advertised id/version, then accepts only bundled/trusted profiles silently or via an approve() hook (else profile_unsupported); bundled set = SPEC/schemas/profiles/social/1.json. PinnedProfile.validateEvent/validateAction compose data-plane validation; AbpValidator = CoreValidator + PinnedProfile. Trust paths (x-abp-trust untrusted/client_authored) enumerated from the pinned profile into profile.trust.{events,actions}[kind] for F4.1/F4.2. Verified via ./init.sh: 39/39 tests pass (25 conformance + 14 profile) + tsc clean. Profile tests cover: social/1 pins silently w/ expected kinds; tampered doc -> profile_mismatch; wrong hash -> profile_mismatch; unknown profile -> profile_unsupported w/o approval, pins w/ approval; invalid doc -> bad_message; composed valid event/action pass; unknown kind + unknown data field + oversize content + world.context>maxProperties + Core-layer (missing seq) all rejected; untrusted/client_authored path enumeration exact.
steps: Load + validate a profile document against core/profile.json meta-schema.
Pin by content hash; reject mismatch (profile_mismatch); approval gate for unknown (id,version,hash).
Compose validation: event/action data validated against pinned profile events[kind].data / actions[kind].data.
Enumerate x-abp-trust:untrusted / client_authored field paths from the pinned profile (feeds F4.1/F4.2).
Unit: bundled abp.social/1 pins silently; tampered doc -> profile_mismatch; unknown kind/field rejected.

features.title: Transport (outbound wss baseline) + envelope (de)serialize
features.evidence: packages/client/src/transport.ts: WssTransport (outbound WebSocket via ws; EventEmitter). One ABP message per UTF-8 JSON frame. connect() resolves on open; send() validates then serializes; inbound #onFrame validates every frame before delivery (size<=65536 -> JSON parse -> validateMessage) and emits 'message'(msg,type) or 'invalid'(reason,errors). TLS posture enforced at construction: wss:// always ok; ws:// only for loopback host; other schemes rejected (§2). Pluggable Validate fn (default CoreValidator; AbpValidator can be swapped in post-pairing). Verified via ./init.sh (exit 0): 10/10 e2e tests on a loopback ws server + tsc clean. Covered: valid hello round-trips and is delivered parsed; unknown-type / oversize / non-JSON / binary inbound frames -> 'invalid' not 'message'; send() refuses invalid + pre-open send throws; ws://non-loopback and non-ws scheme refused; wss://remote accepted. Test runner uses --test-force-exit (loopback servers leave the loop non-empty).
steps: wss client; one message per frame

features.title: Pairing/auth (keypair, challenge-sign, scoped token, resume)
features.evidence: packages/client/src: keypair.ts (Ed25519 via node:crypto; Keypair.generate/fromPem/loadOrCreate[0600 PEM]; raw pubkey as JWK x base64url; sign(nonce)->base64url; verifySignature() for hosts/tests). session.ts (Session: can()/assertCan capability scope, proactive flag, isExpired vs expires_at, authHeader Bearer). envelope.ts (makeEnvelope: randomUUID id + Date.now ts). pairing.ts (pair(): hello->hello_ack pin-by-hash->pair_request->[pair_challenge->sign->pair_request w/ sig]->pair_result; builds scoped Session; asserts pair_result.profile==pinned else profile_mismatch; PairingError carries ABP code; waitFor rejects on error/invalid/timeout; sendResume sends token in envelope.session + last_event_seq). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean; 29/29 client tests (7 keypair + 6 session + 6 pairing + 10 transport). Forgery: tampered sig / wrong-nonce sig / wrong-key sig all rejected by verifySignature; protocol-level host rejection -> pair() throws unauthorized. Scope: can/assertCan + capability gate. Expiry: isExpired boundary. Pinning in handshake: tampered hash -> profile_mismatch; unknown profile w/o approval -> profile_unsupported. Resume: token in envelope + seq cursor in payload, asserted host-side. Happy path challenge->sign->scoped session w/ role+caps+profile.

features.title: Event loop (dispatch events, submit actions, turn deadlines)
features.evidence: packages/client/src/driver.ts: Driver (EventEmitter). start() swaps the transport validator to AbpValidator(pinnedProfile) -> composed data-plane validation now active on the live stream. Dispatches type==event by kind (emit 'event'+'<kind>'), tracks lastEventSeq (resume cursor). Turn-style events (turnKind, default 'turn') -> onTurn handler raced against deadline_ms (relative to receipt); decision action submitted with corr=turn id; null/undefined or timeout -> noop (noopOnTimeout default true) + 'turn_timeout' emitted; settled-guard prevents double-submit. #sendAction enforces session.assertCan (capability scope) + turn.allowed_actions membership before transport.send (which re-validates profile data). act() = proactive action (no corr), requires proactive capability. Also added WssTransport.setValidate(). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean; client 36/36 (incl 7 driver e2e vs in-process mock ws host) + validator 39/39 = 75. Driver e2e: dispatch-by-kind + lastEventSeq tracking; turn->say submitted w/ corr=evt-5; deadline->turn_timeout+noop(corr); action outside allowed_actions -> error, not sent; invalid profile data on the wire -> 'invalid', not dispatched (proves validator swap); proactive emote no corr; act() without proactive cap throws.

features.title: MCP server tools (link/perceive/wait_for_event/say/act/persona_memory)
features.evidence: New package @agent-bridge/mcp. connector.ts: Connector wraps one ABP session (transport+pair+Driver in manual turn mode, noopOnTimeout:false); buffers events for long-poll, caches last perception, tracks turns. Methods link/perceive/waitForEvent/say/act/personaMemory. waitForEvent: drains buffer else registers a waiter w/ timeout (long-poll). say/act submit via driver.respond (corr=current turn) or driver.act (proactive). persona_memory is a local in-memory placeholder (F5.2 = persistent hard-walled store). server.ts: createServer() registers the 6 tools (abp_link/perceive/wait_for_event/say/act/persona_memory) on McpServer w/ zod input schemas, returning JSON text content; tool errors -> isError. bin.ts: stdio entry. Driver refactor (F2.3 carry): added currentTurnId getter + respond()/manual-mode turn settling so the agent (not auto-onTurn) answers turns. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean; mcp 13/13 + client 39/39 + validator 39/39 = 91. MCP tests use a real MCP Client over InMemoryTransport: lists exactly the 6 tools; persona_memory round-trips; abp_link+abp_wait_for_event+abp_perceive drive the FULL stack (MCP client -> server -> connector -> client SDK -> wss -> mock host); pre-link tool call -> isError. Connector e2e (vs mock ws host): link returns role/caps/profile; perceive snapshot; wait_for_event buffered/long-poll/timeout; say correlates to turn (corr=evt-3); act proactive (no corr).
steps: MCP server over the client SDK

features.title: Claude Code restricted profile + docs + manual e2e
features.evidence: packages/mcp/examples/: .mcp.json (registers the agent-bridge stdio server), claude-settings.json (allowlist-only L1 profile: allows only the 6 mcp__agent-bridge__abp_* tools, denies Bash/Read/Edit/Write/NotebookEdit/WebFetch/WebSearch/Task), persona.md (locked untrusted-content system prompt, L2/SPEC §6.2), demo-host.ts (standalone scripted ABP host — a Mira social scene). docs/claude-code.md: full setup (Node 24 type-strip, register server, apply restricted profile, load persona, drive the demo host) + residual-risk note + real-world (P7 host side) pointer. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean (examples now typechecked); mcp 14/14 (incl test/e2e_stdio.test.ts spawning the REAL `node src/bin.ts` over StdioClientTransport, linking to an in-process ABP host and driving a turn -> say arrives with corr=evt-1) + client 39 + validator 39 = 92. Demo host smoke-tested as a subprocess: Connector link -> perception/invite/message/turn -> say -> Mira replies (full conversation loop). NOTE: interactive Claude-Code-clicks-through run is documented for a human operator; the exact stdio path Claude Code uses is proven by the automated e2e + demo-host smoke test.
steps: drive an avatar from Claude Code; evidence

features.title: Untrusted tagging + locked persona prompt + injection eval set
features.evidence: packages/client/src/wrap.ts: wrapUntrusted(data, paths, {source}) delimits the pinned profile's x-abp-trust:untrusted leaves as <untrusted source=...>...</untrusted> before any host text reaches the model; escapeUntrusted HTML-escapes &/</> so content can never forge a closing tag; path walker handles /a/b, [] array items, and object-subtree paths (world.context -> wrapAllStrings wraps string leaves, leaves scalars). Control fields (ids/seq/conversation_id/status) untouched. persona.ts: LOCKED_PERSONA_PROMPT (enforced in-code port of examples/persona.md; data-not-instructions, no-local-powers, delimiter-authoritative clauses) + buildPersonaPrompt(persona) appends curated facts. injection-evals.ts: INJECTION_EVALS (14 cases: 5 exfil + 5 role_override + 4 tool_coercion, incl a </untrusted> delimiter-break). Wired into the MCP Connector #onEvent (L2 choke point): events wrapped via profile.trust.events[kind].untrusted before buffering/perceive. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: 16 schemas parse, tsc clean; validator 39 + client 68 (+8 wrap.test) + mcp 15 (+1) = 122, 0 fail. wrap tests: exact untrusted leaves wrapped + control raw + input not mutated; delimiter-break neutralized (escaped, single real closing tag); world.context object-subtree wraps strings only; missing path no-op; persona clauses present; eval set >=12 all categories unique ids. mcp tests: long-poll content delivered wrapped (source=role:r2), conversation_id raw; injected </untrusted> escaped on the wire.
steps: injection eval: never leak/never call disallowed

features.title: Egress DLP filter (deterministic)
features.evidence: packages/client/src/dlp.ts: EgressFilter (deterministic, high-precision rules: private_key/AWS AKIA-ASIA/openai sk-/github ghp_+pat/slack xox/google AIza/JWT/Bearer/home+windows path/large_base64; plus optional maxLength->oversize). modes block (fail-closed) | redact ([REDACTED:type]). applyEgress(filter, clientAuthoredPaths, data) scans the profile's x-abp-trust:client_authored leaves (path walker supports /a/b and []). RateLimiter (sliding window). Wired into the single outbound choke point Driver.#sendAction: rate-limit -> egress on client_authored paths (block throws / redact sets data.redacted=true) -> send. DLP is ON BY DEFAULT (new EgressFilter(), block mode) so Connector.say (MCP) is protected for free; pass egress:null to disable. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean; client 60/60 (17 dlp unit + 4 driver DLP e2e), mcp 14, validator 39 = 113. dlp tests: each secret type detected; clean prose -> no findings; redact preserves surroundings; applyEgress block/redact/clean; RateLimiter window. driver tests: default-block refuses a say leaking ghp_ token (error, not sent); redact mode rewrites AKIA + sets redacted; clean text passes untouched; rate limit max=1 throws on 2nd.
steps: scan say.text/emote for secrets/keys/JWT/cloud creds/abs paths/large base64

features.title: Capability isolation enforcement (+ optional isolation_mode: process)
features.evidence: packages/client/src/capability.ts: CapabilityGuard (default-deny). ABP_TOOL_NAMES (6, match server.ts) + AVATAR_MCP_ALLOWLIST (mcp__agent-bridge__abp_*) are the only allowed tools (bare or qualified); isAllowed/assertAllowed refuse Bash/Read/Edit/Write/WebFetch/WebSearch/Task, foreign MCP (mcp__filesystem__*), and unknown abp_* — CapabilityError carries the tool. The settings allowlist now DERIVES from AVATAR_MCP_ALLOWLIST (single source of truth). packages/client/src/isolation.ts: isolatedEnv(allow) scrubs parent secrets (only BASE_ENV_KEYS + allow survive); launchIsolated() spawns a child with that scrubbed env (isolation_mode: process, the opt-in OS-level hardening). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: tsc clean; validator 39 + client 74 (+6 capability.test) + mcp 16 (+1 settings.test) = 129, 0 fail. capability tests: every ABP tool allowed bare+qualified; 12 out-of-allowlist names refused + throw; assertAllowed carries tool; extra grants; isolatedEnv drops SECRET_TOKEN keeps PATH honors allow; launchIsolated smoke — child env excludes parent ABP_TEST_SECRET, keeps PATH, live pid. mcp settings.test: examples/claude-settings.json permissions.allow == AVATAR_MCP_ALLOWLIST and denies Bash/Read/Edit/Write/WebFetch. Runtime enforcement point (autopilot forwarding) is wired in F6.2.
steps: avatar context exposes only allowlisted tools

features.title: Prompt-injection regression eval harness
features.evidence: packages/client/src/injection-harness.ts: runInjectionHarness() scores every INJECTION_EVALS case via its machine-readable probe against the real defense stack — wrap probe -> L2 wrapNeutralizes (delimited+escaped, single closing tag); dlp probe -> EgressFilter.inspect(secret) finds it; capability probe -> CapabilityGuard refuses the tool. assertInjectionGate(threshold=1.0) throws InjectionGateError below threshold = the CI gate (runs inside npm test, which init.sh runs across all workspaces). defenses toggles prove the gate bites. Added InjectionProbe to injection-evals.ts (wrap x7 incl delimiter-break + system-prompt/config; dlp x3 = openai key / openssh private key / abs home path; capability x4 = Bash/Read/WebFetch/mcp__filesystem__write_file). Optional runLiveInjectionEval(ask) for behavioral coverage, OFF by default (not in the gate). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 81 (+7 injection-harness.test) + mcp 16 = 136, 0 fail. Tests: all-on -> 100% defended; each defense covers >=1 case; disabling wrap/dlp/capability each drops the rate and fails EXACTLY that probe's cases; assertInjectionGate green passes, throws InjectionGateError when any defense disabled.

features.title: Persona profile format + loader
features.evidence: packages/client/src/persona-profile.ts: loadPersonaProfile(doc) validates a closed curated shape (displayName required; backstory/traits/goals/speakingStyle optional; PERSONA_LIMITS lengths+counts; unknown fields/wrong types/oversize rejected) AND runs an EgressFilter scan so any secret/key/abs-path in the profile is refused (persona must be public/curated). loadPersonaProfileFile(path) for JSON files. buildPersonaPrompt already composes it after the locked prompt. Verified: client 88/88 (+7 persona-profile.test), 0 fail. Tests: valid profile loads; missing displayName rejected; unknown field rejected; oversize backstory + non-array traits + too-many traits rejected; sk- key and /Users/.../id_rsa in backstory rejected; buildPersonaPrompt prepends locked prompt; null/string/array inputs rejected.

features.title: Local social memory store (namespaced, hard-walled)
features.evidence: packages/client/src/memory.ts: PersonaMemoryStore(namespace,{dir}) — persistent JSON file per namespace (mode 0600), get/set/delete/list. safeNamespace() flattens to one safe filename segment (path separators stripped; pure '.'/'..' rejected) so a store can never traverse out of its dir or read another namespace / the main agent's memory; never transmitted to host (closed action schemas carry no memory field). Wired into mcp Connector: created on link() namespaced by the bound role id (session.role.id); lazy 'default' namespace for pre-link; reset on close() so a re-link rebinds. Connector gains ConnectorOptions.memoryDir; tests pass a temp dir. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 93 (+5 memory.test) + mcp 16 = 148, 0 fail. memory tests: set/get/list/delete; persists across reopen; role-a vs role-b hard-walled (b cannot see a's keys); traversal namespace flattened to a single file (no separators) + pure '..' rejected; missing key -> null.
steps: unit: recall town facts; cannot read outside

features.title: Connection daemon (persistent link + reconnect)
features.evidence: packages/client/src/daemon.ts: ConnectionDaemon — start() connects+pairs+drives; on an unexpected transport 'close' it reconnects with exponential backoff (backoffMs, last repeats), re-attaches the Driver (existing session/profile, no re-pair), and sends resume{last_event_seq} so the host backfills/snapshots (§4.3.2). Tracks lastEventSeq as the resume cursor. Events: connected/reconnecting/reconnected/event/error/giveup/stopped. stop() = clean hot-unplug (off close listener, stop driver, close transport, no further reconnect). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 95 (+2 daemon.test) + mcp 16 = 150, 0 fail. daemon e2e vs a resumable mock ws host: stream ticks seq 0/1/2 -> lastEventSeq 2; force socket drop -> daemon reconnects (conns>=2) and sends resume with last_event_seq==2 -> host streams seq 3 -> lastEventSeq 3. stop() test: after stop no 'reconnecting' fires and no new host connection.

features.title: Autopilot loop driving sandboxed headless local agent
features.evidence: packages/mcp/src/autopilot.ts: Autopilot(connector,{brain,guard,maxTurns}) runs an autonomous loop — waitForEvent(turn) -> brain(event) -> route each ToolCall. The brain is injected (production: a sandboxed headless Claude Code launched via isolation_mode:process + the restricted allowlist; tests: a deterministic fn). Runtime defenses at the route choke point: L1 CapabilityGuard.assertAllowed(tool) refuses any out-of-allowlist tool (Bash/Read/foreign-MCP) — this is the runtime enforcement F4.3 deferred; L3 egress DLP runs inside connector.say (Driver #sendAction) so a secret in a say is blocked; L2 wrapping already applied upstream (events the brain sees are wrapped untrusted). Records a transcript {turnSeq,tool,ok,detail}. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 95 + mcp 19 (+3 autopilot.test) = 153, 0 fail (autopilot stable across 3 repeat runs). Tests vs a turn-driving mock host: drives N=5 turns autonomously, each answered with a say (5 ok transcript + 5 host say actions, text increments); brain asking for Bash -> refused at runtime (never reaches host), follow-up noop ok; brain say leaking a ghp_ token -> DLP blocked (never reaches host).
steps: drive sandboxed headless Claude Code via MCP+allowlist
e2e: autonomous N turns, DLP active

features.title: Config + hot-unplug kill switch
features.evidence: packages/mcp/src/avatar.ts: AvatarController(connector,{mode}) — config toggle setMode('in_the_loop'|'autopilot') (switching away from autopilot stops the loop; emits 'mode'); startAutopilot() only valid in autopilot mode; kill(reason) = hot-unplug: stop the loop, send a graceful `bye`, connector.close(), emit 'killed' — idempotent, and post-kill setMode/startAutopilot throw. Added Connector.bye(reason) (best-effort graceful bye envelope; never throws). Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 95 + mcp 22 (+3 avatar.test) = 156, 0 fail. Tests: mode toggles emit in order, wrong-mode startAutopilot refused; kill sends bye (host records reason) + connector.linked false + idempotent + post-kill guards throw + perceive throws not-linked; kill while autopilot waiting resolves the run promptly (close() resolves the pending waiter) and running=false.

features.title: Generic host SDK (expose roles over ABP)
features.evidence: New package @agent-bridge/host (depends only on validator + ws; verifyEd25519 reimplemented via node:crypto so it does NOT depend on the client). AbpHost: WSS server; pins its own World Profile (hash) and validates ALL inbound via AbpValidator; control plane hello/hello_ack(inlines profile+hash)/roles_query/roles_list/pair_challenge+verify-sig/pair_result(mint role+profile+capability-scoped token)/resume/ping/bye; emit(roleId,event) assigns monotonic per-session seq, buffers (ring) for resume, records open turn allowed_actions; inbound action enforced server-side (capabilities + open turn allowed_actions + proactive + size) -> onAction hook; resume backfills buffered events past the cursor or calls onResume for a snapshot on a gap; never blocks on an absent client (emit no-ops if unbound; role unbinds on socket close, token stays valid for resume). Default bind = open roles get all profile action kinds + proactive; claim_required/closed honored. Verified ./init.sh exit 0: validator 39 + client 95 + mcp 21 + host 5 = 160, 0 fail. host e2e (REAL client SDK <-> REAL host, two endpoints one process): handshake+turn+say delivered to onAction with corr; resume backfills buffered seq past a cursor then keeps driving; forged pairing signature -> unauthorized; allowed_actions enforced server-side -> forbidden; unknown message type -> bad_message.
steps: server-side helper: emit events, validate+apply actions, scoped tokens

features.title: AI-Town host adapter (in ai-town repo): controller=external + action queue + wire host SDK
steps: external agents enqueue instead of calling LLM
wire ABP host SDK; e2e drive an AI-Town role

features.title: Codex adapter (+ OpenClaw/Hermes once MCP confirmed)
